_comment: Curated list of mixin handler methods that FATALLY crash on the target MC and
cannot be repaired by remapping. Retromod surgically removes the listed handler
method(s) from the mixin during transformation, so the mixin framework never
processes them - the mod loads and the one feature that handler implemented is
inert, instead of the whole game crashing.
This is the escape hatch for failures we cannot safely auto-detect - most notably
MixinExtras @WrapOperation / @ModifyExpressionValue handlers that capture a @Local
from a vanilla method whose local-variable layout changed between MC versions. The
@Local then resolves to the wrong slot and MixinExtras emits an invalid bridge
(VerifyError: Bad local variable type) at class-load time.
Users can extend or override this via config/retromod/mixin-blocklist.json (same
format). Entries from both files are merged.
Format: each entry needs a 'mixin' (the mixin class, '/' or '.' separated). If
'methods' is given, only those handler methods are stripped (surgical, preferred).
If 'methods' is omitted/empty, every injector handler on the class is stripped.
Add '"strip": "class"' to neutralize the WHOLE mixin instead of stripping
handlers - Retromod repoints the @Mixin annotation at a non-existent target so
the framework skips it entirely (the harmless 'target not found' path). Use this
for mixins that ADD AN INTERFACE to their target (handler-stripping leaves the
interface unapplied → ClassCastException downstream, #68) or that have an
interdependent critical @Inject failure where partial stripping leaves orphans (#69).
RETIRED in 1.3.0 (now REAL translations, not soft-fails): #48 Darker Depths
PlayerMixin save-data (ValueIO adapter, repair-or-strip); #69 Revamped Phantoms
SweepAttackMixin doHurtTarget (the @At signature-drift rewrite); #50 Revamped
Phantoms PhantomMixin getDefaultDimensions (the FlyingMob->Mob owner-alias redirect -
getDefaultDimensions IS still declared by Phantom on 26.x, calling Mob.getDefaultDimensions
at the wrapped expression, so the @ModifyExpressionValue re-resolves).
RETIRED in 1.3.0 (Yung's API worldgen, NoiseChunkMixin ONLY): the whole-
class strip of NoiseChunkMixin is retired. It is repaired by MixinShadowFieldDemotion:
its @Shadow @Final noiseSettings (the field the 1.21.5 worldgen refactor deleted from
NoiseChunk) is demoted to a @Unique field and seeded from the NoiseSettings ctor param
the mixin's <init> handler already captures, so YungsApi's aquifer-override feature works
again. VERIFIED on a NeoForge 26.2 dedicated server: yungsapi loads and 49 force-loaded
chunks generate cleanly (Done reached, zero 'Exception generating new chunk'). Its
attach-to-beardifier handler is an 'instanceof EnhancedBeardifierData' no-op while
BeardifierMixin stays stripped (below), so the two are separable in practice.
STILL STRIPPED (NOT retired): BeardifierMixin. 1.3.0 research showed it is
byte-translatable (its @Inject targets forStructuresInChunk/compute survive on 26.2) and
it APPLIES cleanly, but the headless worldgen pass proved it is NOT launch-safe: with
BeardifierMixin re-enabled, chunk generation throws 'IllegalStateException: Parent chunk
missing' (net.minecraft.server.level.ChunkMap.applyStep) during initial spawn generation
and the server never reaches Done. Its enhanced cross-chunk terrain adaptation is
incompatible with 26.2's stricter chunk-dependency scheduler - a runtime-behavior break
no bytecode remap fixes (same class as True Darkness #68, but found at launch, not
statically). A/B verified on a real 26.2 server: BOTH-translated crashes; NoiseChunk-only
(this state) and both-stripped both reach Done. So BeardifierMixin + BeardifierAccessor
stay whole-class stripped; the terrain-adaptation feature stays inert; the mod loads and
worldgen runs.

blocked.reason: Deeper and Darker (1.3.0 CORRECTION: the former #28 entry named a fictional 'PaintingItemMixin'/'deeperdarker$decrementStackOnServer' that does NOT exist in the 1.4.1 jar - a stale no-op that left the mod's real drift unhandled. The real classes are HangingEntityItemMixin and PaintingMixin, both verified against the 26.2 jar). appendHoverText is an @Inject(HEAD) whose target HangingEntityItem.appendHoverText was re-signatured on 26.x - the single List<Component> tooltip sink was SPLIT into TooltipDisplay + Consumer<Component> - so the handler descriptor no longer matches (a param split, not a leading insert, so the re-signature engine can't repair it). Worse, its body decodes the painting variant via Painting.VARIANT_MAP_CODEC + CustomData.read, BOTH deleted on 26.x, so even a repaired signature would NoClassDefFoundError at runtime. Strip the handler: the mod loads and only the painting-variant tooltip line is inert. PaintingMixin (below) and the decoration/Painting class-move (Common_1_21_11_to_26_1_ClassMoves) keep the rest of the mod working (getPickResult @Inject applies natively).

Deeper and Darker's @Mixin(Painting). Painting moved to entity/decoration/painting/ (class-move redirect handles the @Mixin target) and its getPickResult @Inject applies natively on 26.x. But Painting.dropItem was re-signatured to dropItem(ServerLevel, Entity) on 26.2 (verified), so the mod's dropItem(Entity, CallbackInfo) @Inject at HEAD fails the captured-param descriptor check (a hard InvalidInjectionException that require=0 does not catch). A leading-ServerLevel re-signature WOULD fit mechanically, but 'dropItem' is a generic name shared across many classes, so adding it to the signature-change table risks false-positives; strip the one handler instead (its custom-drop tweak goes inert), keeping getPickResult live. The now-orphaned lambda$dropItem$0 is harmless dead code.

True Darkness (grondag.darkness, the base mod that True Darkness Biomes depends on). MixinLightTexture does '@Mixin(LightTexture.class) implements LightmapAccess' - it makes the vanilla LightTexture implement darkness's LightmapAccess duck-interface. 1.3.0 research (javap on 26.1+26.2) confirms this is genuinely IMPOSSIBLE to translate, not a mapping issue: class_765/LightTexture was DELETED and redesigned into net/minecraft/client/renderer/Lightmap, whose lightmap is a GPU texture (GpuTexture/GpuTextureView/UBO) produced by a separate LightmapRenderStateExtractor.extract(), NOT the CPU DynamicTexture (class_1043) the mixin @Shadows. All three @Shadow fields are gone: the DynamicTexture field has NO equivalent on Lightmap (genuinely removed, not renamed), and the flicker/dirty fields relocated onto a DIFFERENT class (LightmapRenderStateExtractor), which a @Shadow can't reach from the @Mixin(LightTexture) target. The 'No refMap loaded' message is a red herring - the fields are absent regardless of namespace. A real fix is porting True Darkness to the 26.x render-state/UBO lightmap pipeline, outside a mechanical transformer. Whole-class strip neutralizes it cleanly (darkening feature inert, mod loads). Pairs with the MixinGameRenderer entry below.

True Darkness's MixinGameRenderer.onRenderLevel handler DID apply, and on first level render it casts the vanilla LightTexture to grondag.darkness.LightmapAccess -> ClassCastException (the actual fatal crash in #68, on world load). The cast only fails because MixinLightTexture (above) couldn't add the interface. Even if handler-stripping removed onRenderLevel, darkness's lighting would be half-wired; neutralizing the whole render-mixin pair is the clean soft-fail: True Darkness's darker-lighting feature goes inert but the mod loads and the game runs (vs the current crash-on-world-create). MixinDynamicTexture / MixinEndEffects / MixinNetherEffects are left alone - Effects already skip gracefully (their target inner classes moved), DynamicTexture applies fine.

Yung's API's enhanced terrain-adaptation. BeardifierMixin does '@Mixin(Beardifier.class) implements EnhancedBeardifierData' (ADDS an interface) plus @Unique enhanced-iterator fields and two @Inject handlers (supportCustomTerrainAdaptations on forStructuresInChunk, and calculateDensity on compute). 1.3.0 research confirmed it is byte-TRANSLATABLE: both @Inject targets survive on 26.2 (forStructuresInChunk + compute present and matching), its fields are all @Unique (it never shadows the renamed pieces/junctions), and it APPLIES cleanly on a 26.2 server (no mixin-apply failure, no AbstractMethodError). BUT the headless worldgen pass proved it is NOT launch-safe: with BeardifierMixin active, chunk generation throws 'java.lang.IllegalStateException: Parent chunk missing' at net.minecraft.server.level.ChunkMap.applyStep during INITIAL spawn generation, and the dedicated server never reaches Done (the crash is a pure-vanilla chunk-scheduler stack, no yungsapi frame - the enhanced cross-chunk terrain adaptation reads neighbouring chunks in an order 26.2's stricter chunk-dependency scheduler rejects). This is a runtime-behaviour incompatibility, not a mapping problem, so no bytecode remap fixes it (same 'genuinely un-translatable' class as True Darkness #68, but discovered at launch rather than statically). A/B VERIFIED on a real NeoForge 26.2 dedicated server: both-mixins-translated crashes with Parent-chunk-missing; NoiseChunk-only-translated (BeardifierMixin stripped) AND both-stripped BOTH reach Done and generate 49 force-loaded chunks cleanly. So BeardifierMixin stays whole-class stripped: the enhanced terrain-adaptation feature is inert, Beardifier falls back to vanilla structure-blending, and the mod loads and worldgen runs. NoiseChunkMixin (retired above) is separable - its attach-to-beardifier handler is an 'instanceof EnhancedBeardifierData' no-op while this mixin is stripped.

DEAD-CODE companion of BeardifierMixin (which stays whole-class stripped - see above). @Accessor getPieceIterator()/getJunctionIterator() target Beardifier's pieceIterator/junctionIterator fields, which 26.2 replaced with the pieces/junctions Lists, so the accessors can't bind and resolve to abstract methods -> AbstractMethodError if ever called. Verified zero .class references anywhere in the yungsapi jar (5.1.6), so this interface is never used; whole-class stripping it prevents the framework from failing to bind its unresolvable accessors.

DEAD-CODE companion of NoiseChunkMixin (repaired by MixinShadowFieldDemotion, not stripped - see the RETIRED note above). Same removed-field target class on 26.x; verified zero .class references in the yungsapi jar (5.1.6), so stripping the unused accessor is harmless and avoids an unbindable @Accessor. (Kept stripped even though NoiseChunkMixin itself is now translated - the accessor targets the renamed pieces/junctions-style internals and is genuinely dead.)
